key_insight: LiL-Q turns nonlinear PDE solving into a sequence of convex linear least-squares problems by combining quasilinearization with physics-informed neural networks whose parameters enter linearly.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, stable training-free surrogate solvers for difficult PDEs, with strong potential for simulation workflows in fluids, elasticity, and porous media.
method: Apply Bellman-Kalaba quasilinearization to linearize the nonlinear PDE around the current iterate.
Discretize each linear subproblem by collocation in a Linear-in-Learnables trial space and solve with direct QR least squares.
Use LiL bases such as random features, spectral polynomials, or trigonometric expansions to avoid nonconvex gradient-based PINN training.
evidence_signal: The paper reports seven benchmark tests, with convergence in single-digit outer iterations in most cases and machine-precision recovery when the exact solution lies in the trial space.
watch_item: The guaranteed accuracy is limited by the trial-space approximation error and local convergence requires a smallness condition, so performance may depend on a good basis choice and an adequate initial iterate.
paper_title: A Convex Quasilinearization Method for Solving Nonlinear PDEs with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
